description:
Your role: You are a cloud-architect who has just completed a batch migration of legacy micro-services to a new domain.  
    The operations team produced a comma-separated mapping file that lists, line-by-line, which service URL was moved to which new URL.

    Location of the mapping file  
      • /home/user/service_mappings.csv

    Pre-populated contents of /home/user/service_mappings.csv  
      (first line is a header, each subsequent line is a single record)
        Service,OldURL,NewURL
        auth,https://old.example.com/auth,https://api.new-corp.io/auth
        billing,https://old.example.com/bill,https://api.new-corp.io/billing
        catalog,https://old.example.com/catalog,https://api.new-corp.io/catalog
        inventory,https://old.example.com/inv,https://api.new-corp.io/inventory
        shipping,https://old.example.com/ship,https://api.new-corp.io/shipping

    Task goal  
      1. Read /home/user/service_mappings.csv.  
      2. For every data row (skip the header) emit the text:

           <Service> migrated from <OldURL> to <NewURL>

         using single spaces exactly as shown and terminating each entry with a newline character “\n”.  
      3. Redirect **all** of that formatted output into a new file named
           /home/user/migration_report.txt

    Constraints  
      • Perform the whole transformation using only a single awk OR sed command (pipes allowed if you still keep to one awk or one sed invocation).  
      • Do not manually type each line; derive them from parsing the CSV.  
      • The created file /home/user/migration_report.txt must contain **exactly** five lines (one for each service) and nothing else—no leading/trailing blank lines or spaces.

    Exact target file content (shown here for clarity—your generated file must match this byte-for-byte):

        auth migrated from https://old.example.com/auth to https://api.new-corp.io/auth
        billing migrated from https://old.example.com/bill to https://api.new-corp.io/billing
        catalog migrated from https://old.example.com/catalog to https://api.new-corp.io/catalog
        inventory migrated from https://old.example.com/inv to https://api.new-corp.io/inventory
        shipping migrated from https://old.example.com/ship to https://api.new-corp.io/shipping

    Verification  
      After your command runs, the automated grader will read /home/user/migration_report.txt and compare it byte-for-byte with the expected content above. Any deviation (extra spaces, missing newline at EOF, etc.) will fail the test.

    Deliverable  
      • /home/user/migration_report.txt created with the exact contents specified.  
      • A single terminal command (visible in shell history/STDOUT) that accomplished the transformation using awk or sed.

    Hints (non-exhaustive)  
      • Remember to skip the header row.  
      • In awk, FS="," is helpful.  
      • In sed, you can use NR>1 to ignore the first line and capture groups for the three comma-separated fields.  
      • You do NOT need sudo privileges.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:
      • /home/user/service_mappings.csv exists with the 6 lines exactly as listed in the task description, each terminated by a single UNIX newline (LF), and no trailing blank line.
      • No file named /home/user/migration_report.txt is present.

    Expected final filesystem state for successful completion:
      • /home/user/migration_report.txt must exist, be regular file, world-readable, with exact byte content:

auth migrated from https://old.example.com/auth to https://api.new-corp.io/auth
billing migrated from https://old.example.com/bill to https://api.new-corp.io/billing
catalog migrated from https://old.example.com/catalog to https://api.new-corp.io/catalog
inventory migrated from https://old.example.com/inv to https://api.new-corp.io/inventory
shipping migrated from https://old.example.com/ship to https://api.new-corp.io/shipping
<EOF>

      (There is a single newline character after the word “shipping” and no additional blank lines.)
name: task_000000_0cb8130c
